name: Italian 8-layer Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Cold butter or margarine (cut into pieces)
recipeInstructions:
Filling:
1 Large Red sweet pepper, roasted and cut into strips
1 Package (10-ounces) Frozen chopped spinach, thawed
1 Large Fennel bulb, trimmed and coarsely chopped (about 1 cup)
2 Medium Leeks, thinly sliced (about 2/3 cup)
2 tablespoons Olive oil
1 Cup Ricotta cheese
1 Cup Shredded mozzarella (4 ounces)
1/4 cup Pesto or Homemade pesto
1/4 cup Grated Parmesan cheese
1 Egg yolk
4 Ounces Salami, coarsely chopped
1. Make the dough: In a food processor, combine flour and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Add butter and process with several on/off turns until mixture resembles coarse cornmeal. Combine egg, egg white, and milk; add to processor and process until a ball forms. (Alternatively, in a mixing bowl, mix flour and salt, then cut in butter until crumbly. Mix in egg mixture until a ball forms.) Cover dough and chill while preparing the filling.
2. Prepare the filling: Cut roasted pepper into strips. Drain spinach, reserving liquid. Cook fennel and leeks in oil for 5-7 minutes or until tender. In a bowl, mix reserved spinach, ricotta, mozzarella, pesto, Parmesan, egg yolk, and a pinch of pepper.
3. Assemble the pie: Roll chilled dough into a 12-inch diameter circle and place it in an 8-inch springform pan (dough overhangs sides by about 2-1/2 inches). Spread half of the fennel mixture on bottom crust. Top with half of the spinach mixture, salami, and pepper strips. Repeat layers.
4. Roll remaining dough into a 12-inch circle and place it on top. Use scraps to decorate if desired. Fold excess bottom crust over top crust and crimp edges to seal. Make slits in top crust for steam escape.
5. Brush with remaining egg yolk. Bake at 375 degrees F (190 degrees C) for about 50 minutes or until golden. Let stand 15-20 minutes before removing from pan. Serve.
To Assemble Ahead: Bake as directed, cool, chill, remove from pan, wrap in foil, and refrigerate up to 2 days. Before serving, unwrap and heat wrapped in foil at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 50-55 minutes or until heated through.
To Freeze Ahead: After baking and chilling, remove from pan, wrap tightly in foil, and freeze up to 1 month. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator, then reheat as directed.
